content: Microsoft just kicked off Build 2026 with a keynote from CEO Satya Nadella and other company leaders. As expected, it was filled with announcements, ranging from new Surface hardware to an always-on personal assistant and updates across Microsoft’s in-house AI models. If you didn’t watch the event live, you can catch up on all the latest news in the roundup below. A mini Surface PC designed for AI development [Image: https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/06/1.jpg?quality=90&strip=all] The Surface RTX Spark Dev Box is geared toward developers who want to run local AI models on their device, serving as a substitute for Qualcomm’s canceled dev kit. It comes equipped with Nvidia’s new Arm-based Spark RTX chip and 128GB of unified memory, along with preinstalled apps like Visual Studio Code and GitHub Copilot. The Surface RTX Spark Dev Box also has a preconfigured version of Windows 11 Pro with dark mode enabled by default, a simplified taskbar, and no widgets. Microsoft hasn’t revealed pricing or the full list of specs yet, but the device will be available in the US later this year. Developer-optimized Windows updates [Image: The new Intelligent Terminal. https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/06/intelligent-terminal.png?quality=90&strip=all] Microsoft is taking steps to make Windows more developer-friendly. That includes the addition of Coreutils, which the company describes as “Linux-like command-line utilities that run natively” on Windows 11. It’s also launching the ability to create, run, and interact with Linux containers through its Windows Subsystem for Linux (WSL), along with a new Intelligent Terminal that provides context to a developer’s preferred AI-powered agent. An OS designed to connect AI agents across devices [Media: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=OO8Z04KMARE] We got a glimpse at Project Solara, an Android-based operating system designed to run agents across a variety of devices. Microsoft partnered with Qualcomm and MediaTek to develop the system, which could eventually work as a companion to a PC or hand off tasks between devices. During the keynote, Microsoft showed off a couple of sample devices that the tech might run on: a desktop hub and a digital badge. Microsoft jumps on the OpenClaw bandwagon with Scout [Media: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=3ff6UtpPQv8] Microsoft is launching an always-on assistant built on OpenClaw, the open-source AI platform that gained popularity earlier this year. The assistant, called Scout, works with Microsoft 365 apps including Outlook, OneDrive, and Microsoft Teams. It’s designed to perform tasks in the background, helping businesses do things like organize calendars, manage expense reporting, write emails, and more. Scout is part of a broader set of “Autopilot” agents that Microsoft plans on launching, each of which will have its own “identity.” For now, Scout is launching in desktop preview for Frontier customers in the US, but Microsoft plans to eventually make it available to more people. Microsoft’s first in-house reasoning AI model Microsoft is pushing ahead with its work to make its own AI models instead of relying on those created by OpenAI. It revealed seven new models during Build, including one that it says is its first reasoning model. MAI-Thinking-1 offers 35 billion active parameters and a 128K context window that Microsoft says is designed for “complex multi-step instructions, long-context reasoning and code generation.” It also announced updates across its models focused on image, voice, and code generation, as well as transcription. Windows adds guardrails for OpenClaw and other AI agents [Image: OpenClaw on Windows. https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/06/windows-11-openclaw.png?quality=90&strip=all] Speaking of OpenClaw, Microsoft is trying to make the AI agents safer to run through something called Microsoft Execution Containers (MXC). This allows developers to set guardrails for what AI agents can access on their devices. Microsoft is also launching an OpenClaw companion app that will allow you to set up your own agent or connect to existing ones, which will run in a sandboxed environment. A more stable quantum computing chip [Image: https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/06/Majorana-2-full-size_2.jpg?quality=90&strip=all] Microsoft revealed its next-gen quantum computing chip, called Majorana 2, which the company says could speed up its progress in the field. The upgraded chip contains qubits — the units of information in quantum computing — that are 1,000 times more accurate. Microsoft attributes the boost in performance to a new material stack that uses lead and other compounds. With the way things are progressing, Microsoft says it could reach its goal of making a practical quantum computer by 2029.
chineseTitle: Microsoft Build 2026：7 个最重要的公告
chineseSummary: 微软刚刚通过首席执行官 Satya Nadella 和其他公司领导人的主题演讲拉开了 Build 2026 的序幕。正如预期的那样，它充满了公告，从新的 Surface 硬件到永远在线的个人助理，以及微软内部人工智能模型的更新。如果您没有观看现场直播，您可以观看所有[...]
chineseContent: 微软刚刚通过首席执行官 Satya Nadella 和其他公司领导人的主题演讲拉开了 Build 2026 的序幕。正如预期的那样，它充满了公告，从新的 Surface 硬件到永远在线的个人助理，以及微软内部人工智能模型的更新。如果您没有观看现场直播，您可以在下面的综述中了解所有最新消息。专为人工智能开发而设计的迷你 Surface PC [图片：https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/06/1.jpg?quality=90&strip=all] Surface RTX Spark Dev Box 面向想要在其设备上运行本地人工智能模型的开发人员，作为高通取消的开发套件的替代品。它配备了 Nvidia 全新的基于 Arm 的 Spark RTX 芯片和 128GB 统一内存，以及预装的应用程序，如 Visual Studio Code 和 GitHub Copilot。 Surface RTX Spark Dev Box 还预配置了 Windows 11 Pro 版本，默认启用深色模式、简化的任务栏，并且没有小部件。微软尚未透露定价或完整规格列表，但该设备将于今年晚些时候在美国上市。开发人员优化的 Windows 更新 [图片：新的智能终端。 https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/06/intelligent-terminal.png?quality=90&strip=all] Microsoft 正在采取措施使 Windows 对开发人员更加友好。其中包括添加了 Coreutils，该公司将其描述为“在 Windows 11 上本机运行的类似 Linux 的命令行实用程序”。它还推出了通过其 Windows Subsystem for Linux (WSL) 创建、运行 Linux 容器并与之交互的功能，以及一个新的智能终端，为开发人员首选的人工智能代理提供上下文。旨在跨设备连接人工智能代理的操作系统
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于 AI 与科技趋势信号，适合用于观察工具选型、产品机会、开发者关注点和技术路线变化。
